_status: machine-drafted v2 scheme, unreviewed — review pass + Aaron line-item pending

playbook_grounding.position: Buyer should assume only a closed, enumerated list of liabilities and should not accept a catch-all assumption of seller obligations.
playbook_grounding.fallback: Buyer assumes only the liabilities expressly listed in Section 4, and all other liabilities of Seller are excluded unless Buyer expressly agrees in a signed amendment.
playbook_grounding.escalation_trigger: Escalate if the assumed-liability language captures unspecified, ordinary-course, or catch-all seller liabilities.

counter_families.expected_redline_text: Buyer assumes only the liabilities expressly listed in Section 4, and all other liabilities of Seller are excluded unless Buyer expressly agrees in a signed amendment.

acceptable.text: Buyer assumes liabilities expressly enumerated in 4 identified schedule sections. No signed amendment adds other obligations. All other Seller liabilities remain excluded. Clerical schedule updates are circulated within 3 days, and post-closing claim notices are due within 15 days after discovery.

Buyer assumes liabilities expressly enumerated in 5 identified schedule sections. No signed amendment adds other obligations. All other Seller liabilities remain excluded. Clerical schedule updates are circulated within 2 days, and post-closing claim notices are due within 60 days after discovery.

Buyer assumes liabilities expressly enumerated in 7 identified schedule sections. The parties' signed amendment also expressly adds the identified obligations. All other Seller liabilities remain excluded. Clerical schedule updates are circulated within 9 days, and post-closing claim notices are due within 45 days after discovery.

Buyer assumes liabilities expressly enumerated in 6 identified schedule sections. The parties' signed amendment also expressly adds the identified obligations. All other Seller liabilities remain excluded. Clerical schedule updates are circulated within 10 days, and post-closing claim notices are due within 30 days after discovery.

unacceptable.text: Buyer assumes liabilities expressly enumerated in 6 identified schedule sections. No signed amendment adds other obligations. Buyer additionally assumes all ordinary-course and other unspecified Seller liabilities. Clerical schedule updates are circulated within 2 days, and post-closing claim notices are due within 45 days after discovery.

Buyer assumes liabilities expressly enumerated in 7 identified schedule sections. No signed amendment adds other obligations. All other Seller liabilities remain excluded. Clerical schedule updates are circulated within 3 days, and post-closing claim notices are due within 60 days after discovery.

Buyer assumes liabilities expressly enumerated in 5 identified schedule sections. No signed amendment adds other obligations. Buyer additionally assumes all ordinary-course and other unspecified Seller liabilities. Clerical schedule updates are circulated within 10 days, and post-closing claim notices are due within 15 days after discovery.

Buyer assumes liabilities expressly enumerated in 8 identified schedule sections. The parties' signed amendment also expressly adds the identified obligations. Buyer additionally assumes all ordinary-course and other unspecified Seller liabilities. Clerical schedule updates are circulated within 9 days, and post-closing claim notices are due within 30 days after discovery.

playbook_grounding.position: A no-reliance disclaimer must not eliminate, waive, or limit fraud claims.
playbook_grounding.fallback: Nothing in the no-reliance clause limits any claim based on Fraud, intentional misrepresentation, or willful misconduct.
playbook_grounding.escalation_trigger: Escalate if no-reliance language can be read to waive or limit fraud-based claims.

liability_cap_applies_to_fraud: express liability cap applies to fraud
defined liability cap applies to fraud
operative liability cap applies to fraud condition

counter_families.expected_redline_text: Nothing in the no-reliance clause limits any claim based on Fraud, intentional misrepresentation, or willful misconduct.

acceptable.text: Nothing in the no-reliance clause eliminates or waives claims based on Fraud, intentional misrepresentation, or willful misconduct. Those preserved claims remain outside every contractual liability cap. The non-reliance schedule may contain 4 pages of identified statements, and a claimant gives fraud-claim notice within 30 days after discovery where practicable.

Nothing in the no-reliance clause eliminates or waives claims based on Fraud, intentional misrepresentation, or willful misconduct. Those preserved claims remain outside every contractual liability cap. The non-reliance schedule may contain 6 pages of identified statements, and a claimant gives fraud-claim notice within 15 days after discovery where practicable.

Nothing in the no-reliance clause eliminates or waives claims based on Fraud, intentional misrepresentation, or willful misconduct. Those preserved claims remain outside every contractual liability cap. The non-reliance schedule may contain 8 pages of identified statements, and a claimant gives fraud-claim notice within 60 days after discovery where practicable.

Nothing in the no-reliance clause eliminates or waives claims based on Fraud, intentional misrepresentation, or willful misconduct. Those preserved claims remain outside every contractual liability cap. The non-reliance schedule may contain 2 pages of identified statements, and a claimant gives fraud-claim notice within 45 days after discovery where practicable.

unacceptable.text: The no-reliance clause bars claims based on extra-contractual Fraud, intentional misrepresentation, and willful misconduct. Those preserved claims remain outside every contractual liability cap. The non-reliance schedule may contain 6 pages of identified statements, and a claimant gives fraud-claim notice within 60 days after discovery where practicable.

Nothing in the no-reliance clause eliminates or waives claims based on Fraud, intentional misrepresentation, or willful misconduct. Any surviving fraud-based claim remains subject to the general contractual liability cap. The non-reliance schedule may contain 4 pages of identified statements, and a claimant gives fraud-claim notice within 15 days after discovery where practicable.

The no-reliance clause bars claims based on extra-contractual Fraud, intentional misrepresentation, and willful misconduct. Any surviving fraud-based claim remains subject to the general contractual liability cap. The non-reliance schedule may contain 2 pages of identified statements, and a claimant gives fraud-claim notice within 30 days after discovery where practicable.

Nothing in the no-reliance clause eliminates or waives claims based on Fraud, intentional misrepresentation, or willful misconduct. Any surviving fraud-based claim remains subject to the general contractual liability cap. The non-reliance schedule may contain 8 pages of identified statements, and a claimant gives fraud-claim notice within 45 days after discovery where practicable.

playbook_grounding.position: Employee matters should bound any buyer offer obligations and should not admit successor liability.
playbook_grounding.fallback: Buyer may decide which employees receive offers, any offers will be on terms determined by Buyer, and nothing in this Agreement creates an admission of successor liability or assumed employment obligations.
playbook_grounding.escalation_trigger: Escalate if Buyer must hire all employees, match all terms, or admit successor liability.

counter_families.expected_redline_text: Buyer may decide which employees receive offers, any offers will be on terms determined by Buyer, and nothing in this Agreement creates an admission of successor liability or assumed employment obligations.

acceptable.text: Buyer may decide which employees, if any, receive offers, and no minimum percentage of Seller's workforce must be hired; terms of any offers remain determined by Buyer. Nothing in the covenant admits successor liability or assumed employment obligations. An offeree receives 7 days to respond, and Buyer may schedule onboarding within 30 days after acceptance.

Buyer may decide which employees, if any, receive offers, and no minimum percentage of Seller's workforce must be hired; terms of any offers remain determined by Buyer. Nothing in the covenant admits successor liability or assumed employment obligations. An offeree receives 10 days to respond, and Buyer may schedule onboarding within 20 days after acceptance.

Buyer may decide which employees, if any, receive offers, and no minimum percentage of Seller's workforce must be hired; terms of any offers remain determined by Buyer. Nothing in the covenant admits successor liability or assumed employment obligations. An offeree receives 3 days to respond, and Buyer may schedule onboarding within 10 days after acceptance.

Buyer may decide which employees, if any, receive offers, and no minimum percentage of Seller's workforce must be hired; terms of any offers remain determined by Buyer. Nothing in the covenant admits successor liability or assumed employment obligations. An offeree receives 5 days to respond, and Buyer may schedule onboarding within 40 days after acceptance.

unacceptable.text: Buyer must offer employment to at least 100% of Seller's employees; terms of those offers otherwise remain determined by Buyer. Nothing in the covenant admits successor liability or assumed employment obligations. An offeree receives 10 days to respond, and Buyer may schedule onboarding within 10 days after acceptance.

Buyer may decide which employees, if any, receive offers, and no minimum percentage of Seller's workforce must be hired; terms of any offers remain determined by Buyer. The covenant constitutes Buyer's admission of successor liability and assumed employment obligations. An offeree receives 7 days to respond, and Buyer may schedule onboarding within 20 days after acceptance.

Buyer must offer employment to at least 75% of Seller's employees; terms of those offers otherwise remain determined by Buyer. The covenant constitutes Buyer's admission of successor liability and assumed employment obligations. An offeree receives 5 days to respond, and Buyer may schedule onboarding within 30 days after acceptance.

Buyer must offer employment to at least 50% of Seller's employees; terms of those offers otherwise remain determined by Buyer. Nothing in the covenant admits successor liability or assumed employment obligations. An offeree receives 3 days to respond, and Buyer may schedule onboarding within 40 days after acceptance.

playbook_grounding.position: Buyer's closing conditions should include the absence of a material adverse effect before closing.
playbook_grounding.fallback: Buyer's obligation to close is conditioned on no Material Adverse Effect having occurred with respect to the Purchased Assets or the acquired business before closing.
playbook_grounding.escalation_trigger: Escalate if Buyer must close despite a material adverse effect.

buyer_may_refuse_closing: operative buyer may refuse closing condition

counter_families.expected_redline_text: Buyer's obligation to close is conditioned on no Material Adverse Effect having occurred with respect to the Purchased Assets or the acquired business before closing.

acceptable.text: No Material Adverse Effect has occurred before closing. Buyer nevertheless remains unconditionally obligated to close. Seller gives notice of a potential Material Adverse Effect within 7 days and updates the closing-condition record every 8 days.

A Material Adverse Effect has occurred with respect to the Purchased Assets or acquired business before closing. Buyer may refuse to close while that closing condition is unsatisfied. Seller gives notice of a potential Material Adverse Effect within 1 days and updates the closing-condition record every 6 days.

No Material Adverse Effect has occurred before closing. Buyer may refuse to close while that closing condition is unsatisfied. Seller gives notice of a potential Material Adverse Effect within 3 days and updates the closing-condition record every 4 days.

A Material Adverse Effect has occurred with respect to the Purchased Assets or acquired business before closing. Buyer may refuse to close while that closing condition is unsatisfied. Seller gives notice of a potential Material Adverse Effect within 5 days and updates the closing-condition record every 2 days.

unacceptable.text: A Material Adverse Effect has occurred with respect to the Purchased Assets or acquired business before closing. Buyer nevertheless remains unconditionally obligated to close. Seller gives notice of a potential Material Adverse Effect within 1 days and updates the closing-condition record every 4 days.

A Material Adverse Effect has occurred with respect to the Purchased Assets or acquired business before closing. Buyer nevertheless remains unconditionally obligated to close. Seller gives notice of a potential Material Adverse Effect within 7 days and updates the closing-condition record every 6 days.

A Material Adverse Effect has occurred with respect to the Purchased Assets or acquired business before closing. Buyer nevertheless remains unconditionally obligated to close. Seller gives notice of a potential Material Adverse Effect within 5 days and updates the closing-condition record every 8 days.

A Material Adverse Effect has occurred with respect to the Purchased Assets or acquired business before closing. Buyer nevertheless remains unconditionally obligated to close. Seller gives notice of a potential Material Adverse Effect within 3 days and updates the closing-condition record every 2 days.

benign_change_templates.text: The parties confirm that Delaware law governs this Agreement.
The parties confirm that signing and closing will occur simultaneously.

novel_deviation_templates.text: Buyer irrevocably waives every claim based on fraud, intentional misrepresentation, or willful misconduct.
